<<<KOT:mythologisation>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"mythologisation is a process of linguistic and cultural transformation (making something into myth or treating it as mythological). It requires concepts like narrative, cultural practice, interpretation, and belief that are not stably expressible in the prime lexicon and are not anchored in the catalog. The everyday grounding would need anthropological or semiotic primitives outside the scope of this tier."}
<<<END:mythologisation>>>>

{"kind":"molecule","gloss":"a kind of body part inside the body; it is not a kind of part that makes electrical things happen; many of these parts help other parts move; people can see these parts only after very small things are used to make them big.","groundingNote":"a kind of {urn:molecule-v0:body|body} [m] part inside the body; not a kind of part that makes electrical things happen; many of these parts help other parts move; people see these parts only after very small things are used to make them big.","groundingRefs":["urn:molecule-v0:body"]}
<<<END:neuroglia>>>>
